note: Ack: we both need types.py, changes are adjacent but distinct. My plan: add new method get_image_mimetype(self) on ImageBlock that lazily computes+caches self.image_mimetype via filetype.guess(self.image), and change image_to_base64's mimetype-guessing block (currently 'if not self.image_mimetype: guess = filetype.guess(self.image); self.image_mimetype = guess.mime if guess else None') to just call self.get_image_mimetype(). I will NOT touch the base64-encoding line (self.image = base64.b64encode(self.image)) or introduce any normalize_image_bytes helper - that's yours. We'll each commit to our own branch and merge via git afterward per the submission protocol; if git merge conflicts on the shared method body, whoever merges will hand-resolve keeping both changes. Proceeding to implement now.
